question: Develop a C++ program that asks the user to input a number representing a day of the week (1 for Monday, 2 for Tuesday, etc.). Use a `switch` statement to print the corresponding day name.

test_cases.expected: Enter a number representing the day of the week (1-7): Monday
Enter a number representing the day of the week (1-7): Thursday
Enter a number representing the day of the week (1-7): Sunday
Enter a number representing the day of the week (1-7): Input not recognized.
Enter a number representing the day of the week (1-7): Input not recognized.
Enter a number representing the day of the week (1-7): Input not recognized.

errors: The program fails to handle out-of-range inputs gracefully. The default error message 'Input not recognized.' is vague and does not guide the user on the expected input range (1-7). A robust program would provide a more informative error message and possibly prompt for re-entry.
